title: JSON Decoding, DTOs, Domain Types, Unknown Fields, and Schema Drift · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed ranger review reconstructs json decoding, dtos, domain types, unknown fields, and schema drift from revision, command, arguments, request identity, response fixture, state transition, cache decision, goroutine ownership, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for typed PokéAPI DTO-to-domain admission boundary after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggin…
objectives: Define narrow transport structs with exact JSON tags, nested shapes, optionality, and only fields needed by the command.
Choose unknown-field tolerance deliberately, bound the body before decoding, reject trailing JSON, and preserve decode causes.
Validate ID, name, dimensions, stats, types, abilities, URLs, and collection bounds before constructing domain values.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A review Go team handles case 2863 during a race-instrumented cache stress case. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile bounded JSON fixtures with typed domain admission and trailing-document rejection; failed and canceled commands must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one catch, owned record, encounter relation, or history state and prove the prior invariant. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define narrow transport structs with exact JSON tags, nested shapes, optionality, and only fields needed by the command.
